papers.name: Paper 3 School-based Assessment (Coursework)
papers.coverage_notes: One extended essay (max 2000 words) on a historical topic.

papers.name: Paper 4 Alternative to Coursework
papers.coverage_notes: Written alternative for centres unable to run Paper 3.

assessment_objectives.name: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding
assessment_objectives.description: Demonstrate knowledge and understanding of historical events, individuals, periods and changes.
classification_hints: Items asking for dates, names, places, terminology, recall of events.

assessment_objectives.name: Explain, analyse and evaluate
assessment_objectives.description: Explain, analyse and evaluate historical events, periods and the work of historians.
classification_hints: Items requiring explanation, analysis of causes/consequences, evaluation of sources or arguments.

assessment_objectives.name: Use historical sources
assessment_objectives.description: Use historical sources to construct historical arguments and reach judgements.
classification_hints: Items involving source analysis, corroboration, judgement about usefulness/reliability.

question_styles.name: Source-based question
question_styles.description: Interpretation, cross-referencing and evaluation of historical sources.

difficulty_ladder.description: Recall of facts, definitions or single-step application.

lesson_use.notes: Set supported=true only when a teacher/student/parent mode is declared and validated.

marking_guidance.command_words_ref: CAIE IGCSE History command words (describe, explain, analyse, evaluate, 'how far do you agree') are defined in Section 4 of the syllabus.
marking_guidance.notes: Single-tier qualification (grades A*-G). Candidates take Paper 3 (Coursework) or Paper 4 (Alternative), not both.
